hero.headline: Builds things that ship.
hero.subline: Full-stack product engineer who covers the whole surface: product design, web, native iOS and macOS, backend, and the production infrastructure under it all.
hero.kicker: Niranjan Rakesh · Full-stack product engineer · UAE

about.lead: Most engineers hand off at the edge of their layer. I don't have one: I design the product, build the web and native apps, write the backend, and run the servers it ships on. Everything on this site is live, in use, and built alone.
body: The day job is engineering at Kharetah, a UAE fintech, with a remit that reads like several jobs: product engineering across its platforms, LLM-powered internal tooling, the Azure cloud everything runs on, the security work that comes with financial data, and the company's IT.

The personal work on this page holds the same standard, in production. A couples app with a zero-dependency Node backend and a native SwiftUI client submitted to the App Store. A self-hosted streaming platform with a custom sync protocol, distributed as a prebuilt Docker image. A signed, notarized macOS utility. Each one designed, built, deployed, and maintained by one person.

I trained in Computer Science and started in design, and it shows at both ends. I'll hand-roll drag-and-drop from raw pointer events or tune an HLS pipeline for iOS quirks, and I'll also be the one who notices the type is wrong. Teams usually hire separate people for those. I'm the overlap.

themes.title: Owns products end to end
themes.detail: One person from first sketch to systemd service: design, web, native iOS and macOS, backend, infrastructure, and the App Store paperwork.

themes.title: Ships, then keeps shipping
themes.detail: Everything here runs in production with real users and a maintenance history: bug fixes from daily use, migrations, uptime to keep.

themes.title: Depth below the framework
themes.detail: Zero-dependency servers, custom sync protocols, raw pointer events, scrypt auth without a library. Goes low-level when the framework is the problem.

smallerBuilds.name: Flixify
smallerBuilds.line: Native macOS drag-and-drop transcoder that preps uploads for PookieFlix: hardware VideoToolbox H.264, bundled ffmpeg, shipped as a signed and notarized DMG.

smallerBuilds.name: PerfectTasks
smallerBuilds.line: Full-stack task manager (Next.js, Drizzle, Clerk) designed, planned, and built test-first in a single day.

smallerBuilds.name: orbit-core
smallerBuilds.line: NestJS + Prisma backend for time management: OAuth, Google Calendar sync, multi-device conflict resolution, BullMQ jobs.

contact.cta: Hiring someone who can ship the whole thing?

footer.line: Designed and built from scratch by Niranjan Rakesh.
